title: chrysothamnus parryi subsp. nevadensis
intro: Chrysothamnus parryi subsp. nevadensis, also known as the Nevada goldenrod, is a flowering plant in the sunflower family that is native to the southwestern United States. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for wound healing and reducing pain. The health benefits of Nevada goldenrod include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for its medicinal uses, although more research is needed to confirm its full potential.
